title: Google Online Security Blog: Cultivating a robust and efficient quantum-safe HTTPS
why:
- Chrome is developing Merkle Tree Certificates (MTCs) to enable HTTPS certificates secure against quantum computers.
- MTCs replace traditional X.509 certificate chains with compact Merkle Tree proofs, reducing bandwidth and performance penalties associated with quantum-resistant algorithms.
- Chrome's rollout plan includes three phases: feasibility testing with Cloudflare, inviting CT Log operators to help bootstrap public MTCs, and creating a dedicated Chrome Quantum-resistant Root Store (CQRS) that only supports MTCs.
- MTCs make certificate transparency a fundamental property, ensuring no certificate can be issued without inclusion in a public tree.
- Chrome is actively collaborating with standards bodies, such as the IETF, to ensure industry-wide adoption and compatibility of quantum-safe HTTPS.
